programmes.show_description: 1/3. Around the release of their ...Yes Please! album, here's Happy Mondays live at the Kilburn National in 1992.
2/3. Featuring a selection of early Happy Mondays sessions from 1986 and 1989 as well as highlights from their 1991 Wembley Arena show.
3/3. Shaun and Bez curate a one-hour Happy Mondays playlist!
4/3. In part 1 Mark Radcliffe looks back at punk scene Mick Hucknall, Pete Shelley & Mike Pickering and in part 2 to the 60s beat boom with the help of Graham Nash.
5/3. Part 3 Factory. Mark Radcliffe talks to Tony Wilson about his unique record label and part 4 focuses on mainstream acts such as 10cc, Simply Red & M People. First broadcast 2001.
Chris chats to musician Thea Gilmore as she returns to the show with the finished track from her interactive art project 'The Echo Line'.
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Lauren gives you the finest blend of fantastic music for your Tuesday morning.
Ahead of their upcoming tour, Craig is joined by punk legend Jake Burns of Stiff Little Fingers to chat about his illustrious career.
Huw handpicks brand-new releases alongside a selection of tracks he's loving right now, plus De La Soul take part in this week's Song I Wish I'd Written.
New London band RIP Magic are live in session from Maida Vale, following the release of their single 5Words.
Marc Riley plays his music loves from every era and genre.
Ahead of their performance at the 6 Music Festival in Greater Manchester, Lynks is this week's Artist in Residence with shows centred on four basic human needs.
Cillian handpicks music by The Beatles, PJ Harvey, Aretha Franklin, Feist and Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds.
Cillian hand-picks music by Irma Thomas, Mac DeMarco, Julie Byrne, John Francis Flynn and Brian Eno.
Want to hear the latest tracks 6 Music have added to their playlist? Then listen here!
Names in Songs returns. Can you hear your name in a well-known song?
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Rebecca Lucy Taylor aka Self Esteem joins around her role as Maggie Frisby in the 50th anniversary of Teeth 'N' Smiles play by Sir David Hare.
Craig celebrates your midweek wins for Wednesday, I'm In Love, and we hear another of your left-field music suggestions as we turn Left At The Lights.
Belfast trio Chalk join Huw in session from Maida Vale ahead of their debut album Crystalpunk coming out this Friday. Plus, we get to know 100 Rabbits in this week's Huw Are You.
Deb chats to rapper, singer and producer Miso Extra about some of her favourite tracks, following the release of her latest single Right Here.
Marc Riley plays his music loves from every era and genre.
Ahead of their performance at the 6 Music Festival in Greater Manchester, Lynks is this week's Artist in Residence with shows centred on four basic human needs.
